data.narrative:
Modern Hydrogen, a Series B-stage climate and energy company that has raised $100M to date, is valued here against a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA) operating in the same sector. The peer set for same-stage analysis comprises 6 climate and energy companies at Series B, ranging from Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B) to CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $100M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wider peer range reflects material dispersion in the climate technology subsector, driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' notably higher valuation relative to peers like Reverion ($310M) and Sage Geosystems ($400M).

Triangulating these two methods produces a median valuation of $272M, with a low-end case of $162M and high-end case of $408M. The valuation range is most sensitive to peer-set composition and stage-comparable selection. The five-fold spread between peer minimum and maximum valuations underscores execution risk and technology-stage heterogeneity within this sector, leaving meaningful uncertainty in any point estimate.
